rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 30, 2025 line 1. PETITIONER NIGEL CHANTER, LAI CHEUNG, PETER BAUMANNN, ALISON BAUMANN, TON-YUN FANG, GNOMES COVE LIMITED PARTNERSHIP, BABAC DOANE Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption Claim of Exemption is OFF CALENDAR. Amended Notice of Hearing on Claim of Exemption filed on January 2, 2025. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MIHAL EMBERTON VS. SAN FRANCISCO CITY GOVERMENT
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To Plaintiff'S Sixth Amended Complaint For Declaratory And Injunctive Relief; Verified Petition For Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 30, 2025 line 2. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Demurrer To Plaintiff's Sixth Amended Complaint For Declaratory And Injunctive Relief; Verified Petition For Writ Of Mandate Defendant's Demurrer to Sixth Amended Complaint is SUSTAINED with and without leave to amend, as follows: The First Cause of Action for Exhaustion of Administrative Remedies is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff's claim that the City made Anti-blight decisions to search her private property and file violation notices does not state a claim for relief. Plaintiff must state facts demonstrating she exhausted her administrative remedies regarding the City's Anti-blight enforcement decisions. The Second Cause of Action for Exhaustion of Administrative Remedies is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff's claim that Anti-blight enforcement's administrative remedies are inadequate does not state a claim for relief. Plaintiff must state facts demonstrating she exhausted her administrative remedies regarding the City's Anti-blight enforcement decisions. The Third Cause of Action for Substantive Due Process is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff's claim for denial of substantive due process based on the City's exercise of discretion does not state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action. Plaintiff has not alleged a protected property interest. The Fourth Cause of Action for Procedural Due Process is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff's must state facts demonstrating how she was denied procedural due process. The Fifth Cause of Action for violation of Equal Protection is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must state facts demonstrating she was a member of a suspect class or group. The Sixth Cause of Action for Negligence is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must allege sufficient facts to constitute a claim for negligence. The Seventh Cause of Action for Fraud is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must allege sufficient facts to constitute a claim for fraud demonstrating how, when, where, to whom and by what means fraudulent representations were made. The Eighth Cause of Action for Extortion and Robbery is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff failed to state a cause of action for extortion and robbery. California Penal Code sections 518 et seq. & 211 et seq. do not create a private right of action. The Ninth Cause of Action for Conflicts of Interest is sustained with leave to amend to state facts constituting a proper basis for relief. California's Business and Professions Code section 6068 does not create a private right of action. Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice of Court orders and other exhibits is granted. Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ALISON THOMAS VS. ERNEST ANTONIS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 30, 2025 line 5. DEFENDANTS ERNEST ANTONIS, MAKRAS REAL ESTATE, VICTOR MAKRAS'S DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT. Defendant's Demurrer to First Amended Complaint is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to state any viable claims against the Defendants in clear and concise language; complaint must include a demand for judgment; each cause of action must be clearly labeled and indicate which defendant it is alleged against; exhibits must be clearly labeled and referenced in the complaint by its designated label (e.g. Ex A-Z or Ex. 1-20). Parties must comply with all procedural rules regarding drafting the pleadings (e.g. CCP 425.10; CRC 2.112) and regarding the format of briefs (CRC 3.1113). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
